title: DOM Manipulation Mastery
tagline: Create and control HTML elements with code.

accomplishments: Stop writing HTML by hand — create it with JavaScript!
Build interactive comment sections that grow dynamically
Add and remove elements on the fly

do.title: Build a Dynamic Comment Section
steps: Add comments and see them appear instantly
Delete comments by clicking the × button
Notice: NO hardcoded comments in HTML!
do.code:
💬 Dynamic Comments

Comments (0)

do.result: You created elements with JavaScript! Comments appear without any hardcoded HTML.
do.proofRequired: Screenshot showing at least 3 comments created dynamically.

concepts.name: What is the DOM?
concepts.definition: DOM (Document Object Model) is JavaScript's representation of your HTML. It's a tree structure where every HTML element is an object you can manipulate.
concepts.analogy: Think of HTML as a blueprint and the DOM as the actual building. JavaScript can rearrange rooms (elements) without redrawing the blueprint.

concepts.name: document.createElement()
concepts.definition: Creates a new HTML element in memory (not yet on the page). Syntax: const el = document.createElement('div'). The element exists but is invisible until you add it to the page.
concepts.analogy: Building furniture in a workshop. It's real, but nobody sees it until you carry it into the room.

concepts.name: appendChild() and append()
concepts.definition: Adds a child element to a parent. parent.appendChild(child) adds it as the last child. Modern parent.append(child) does the same and can add multiple at once.
concepts.analogy: Stacking books onto a shelf — each new one goes on top of the pile.

concepts.name: insertBefore() and prepend()
concepts.definition: Place an element before another. parent.insertBefore(newEl, referenceEl) is the classic method. parent.prepend(newEl) is the modern shortcut for adding at the start.
concepts.analogy: Cutting in line at a specific spot — you get to choose exactly where to stand.

concepts.name: Element properties: textContent, className, dataset
concepts.definition: Set text with el.textContent = 'Hello'. Set classes with el.className = 'card' or el.classList.add('active'). Store custom data with el.dataset.userId = '123'.
concepts.analogy: Labeling a box: textContent is the contents, className is the color stripe, dataset is the hidden inventory tag.

concepts.name: innerHTML vs createElement
concepts.definition: innerHTML is fast to write but vulnerable to XSS with user input and destroys event listeners. createElement is safer, preserves listeners, and gives full control. Use createElement for user-generated content.
concepts.analogy: innerHTML is like photocopying a whole page. createElement is like typing each word by hand — slower but precise and safe.

concepts.name: Removing elements and event delegation
concepts.definition: el.remove() deletes an element from the DOM. For many dynamic children, add ONE listener to the parent and check e.target — this is 'event delegation' and handles current AND future elements.
concepts.analogy: Instead of giving every student their own teacher, one teacher watches the whole class — much more efficient.

understand.keyTakeaway: createElement() builds elements safely. appendChild() adds them to the page. Use data attributes to store information, and event delegation to handle clicks on dynamic elements.

build.goal: Build a Dynamic Task Manager where you add tasks, mark them complete, and delete them — all with createElement
build.hint: Use dataset.taskId to store the task ID on each element so you can find it later with querySelector!

challenge.title: Build a Dynamic Photo Gallery with Lightbox
challenge.mission: Create a photo gallery that loads images dynamically, creates thumbnail elements, and opens a lightbox when clicked — all using createElement and DOM manipulation.
requirements: Array of photo objects with: url, title, description
Create thumbnail grid dynamically (no hardcoded images!)
Each thumbnail: image, title overlay, click handler
Lightbox shows: full image, title, description, close button
Keyboard support: Esc to close, arrow keys to navigate
Create ALL elements with createElement (NO innerHTML!)
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add a 'Like' button to each photo that shows a heart icon and tracks likes using localStorage!

capabilities: Add elements to the page with appendChild() and append()
Remove elements with remove() and removeChild()
Set element properties: textContent, className, id, attributes
Use data attributes to store custom information
Add event listeners to dynamically created elements
Build complete interfaces without hardcoded HTML

bridge.preview: Excellent! You can now create and manipulate DOM elements dynamically. In the next lesson, you'll learn advanced DOM techniques — querySelector, traversing the DOM tree, manipulating classes and styles, and building interactive components.
